intro: Lomelosia pseudograminifolia, also known as the false grass Lomelosia, is a flowering plant species in the borage family (Boraginaceae) that is native to the Mediterranean region. Traditionally, the plant is used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, inflammation, and respiratory conditions, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Lomelosia pseudograminifolia include flavonoids, such as 3,7-dihydroxy-4'-methoxyflavone, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal activities. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are available in some health food stores and online marketplaces, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
